info.title: Capafy User Agent API
info.description:
Capafy User Agent API (user-facing Agent API, intended for external developers / Agent callers).

## General conventions
- Unless stated otherwise, every response uses the unified envelope `R<T>`: `code` (business code, 0 means success), `msg` (message), `data` (payload).
- Authentication uses an Access Token (prefix `am_sk_`), supplied either as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` or `X-Access-Token: <token>`.
- `/auth/**` requires no authentication; all other endpoints require an Access Token.
- Amounts and prices are in USD; the currency is currently fixed to `usd`.

## Authentication lifecycle
1. `POST /auth/login` with an email to send a one-time code (OTP) and receive a `challengeId`.
2. `POST /auth/login/verify` with `challengeId` + `code` to obtain `data.accessToken`.
3. Persist `data.accessToken` securely and send it as the Bearer token (or `X-Access-Token`) on every subsequent `/agent/**` request.

## Exception: the 04_Chat module
The following endpoints do **not** use the `R<T>` envelope; they return `text/event-stream` or raw JSON:
- `POST /agent/relay/instances/{instanceId}/messages` (send message and open the SSE stream)
- `POST /agent/relay/instances/{instanceId}/messages/reconnect` (SSE reconnect)
- `POST /agent/relay/instances/{instanceId}/interrupt` (interrupt task)
- `GET  /agent/relay/instances/{instanceId}/messages` (pull offline messages)

SSE events have only three `type` values: `message` / `timeout` / `done`.

x-api-lifecycle.deprecation-policy: Backward-incompatible (breaking) changes are announced at least 90 days in advance via the changelog and email to registered developers, and the superseded version stays available throughout that window. We maintain backward compatibility wherever possible; additive, non-breaking changes (new endpoints, new optional fields/parameters) may ship at any time without prior notice and without a version bump.
x-api-lifecycle.versioning-strategy: Major versions are carried in the path prefix. The current endpoints are unversioned and are treated as v1; they will remain available unchanged when a future major version ships under a new prefix (e.g. /v2/). For finer-grained control within a major version, clients may optionally pin behavior with a dated 'Api-Version: YYYY-MM-DD' request header; omitting it selects the latest stable behavior of that major version.

post.description: Sends a one-time code (OTP) to the email and returns a `challengeId`.

post.summary: Verify OTP and complete Agent login
post.description:
Verifies the email OTP, completes Agent login, and returns an `accessToken`.
- In the Agent scope, `source` is fixed to `agent`.
- If the email is not yet registered, the user, wallet, and a default accessToken are created automatically.
- The `challengeId` is invalidated immediately whether verification succeeds or fails.

get.description:
Returns the currently published, publicly visible version detail for `agentId`
(only returned when the Agent is `online` and has a latest published version).
Does not return internal fields such as credential config, audit status, package URL, or timestamps.

default.description: Business error. Typical codes: `400` (blank agentId), `2004` AGENT_NOT_FOUND, `2010` AGENT_NOT_AVAILABLE.

post.description:
Calls the retrieval service (Capafy) with a natural-language demand description, returns matching Agents,
and enriches each with local display fields and a relevance score.
No request body; parameters are passed via query. Price filtering applies only when both `minPrice` and `maxPrice` are present.

parameters.name: minPrice
parameters.description: Minimum price; effective only when sent together with maxPrice

parameters.name: maxPrice
parameters.description: Maximum price; effective only when sent together with minPrice

post.description:
The current user reviews the specified Agent. Requires a valid purchase record; one review per user per Agent.
A `deleted` previous review is revived and updated; a `violation` previous review cannot be re-published.

default.description:
Business error. Typical codes: `2004` AGENT_NOT_FOUND, `4048` AGENT_REVIEW_PURCHASE_REQUIRED,
`4049` AGENT_REVIEW_ALREADY_EXISTS, `4050` AGENT_REVIEW_VIOLATION, `400` (rating/comment validation failed).

post.description:
Place a new order or renew. Always paid with `credit`.
- New order: `agentId` is required; `hours` is required for hourly plans; `billingLineNo` selects a billing plan (0/1/2, default 0).
- Renewal: passing `instanceId` switches to renewal mode. Hourly-instance renewal requires `hours`; expired-subscription renewal omits `hours`.

default.description: Business error (invalid params, insufficient balance, unsupported pricing, invalid renewal target, etc.). Idempotency: `400` BAD_REQUEST when the same `Idempotency-Key` is reused with a different request body; `429` TOO_MANY_REQUESTS when the first request for that key is still being processed (retry later with the same key).

get.description: Returns the current user's transaction order / invoice list (subscriptions return invoices). Not paginated, sorted by creation time descending.

Returns a single order / invoice detail. `orderId` supports the `buy-`/`hou-`/`cre-`/`str-` prefixes.
For subscriptions this returns the first-invoice detail; do not pass a `subscriptionId` from other subscription endpoints as `orderId`.

default.description: Business error (orderId not found / not owned by the current user, etc.)

post.description:
Creates a Credits top-up order and returns a Stripe Checkout link. Currently `1 USD = 1 Credit`,
with a minimum top-up threshold of `10` USD (enforced at the service layer). Credits are added after the Stripe webhook confirms payment.

default.description: Business error. Typical codes: `400` BAD_REQUEST (amount below 10 USD, or same `Idempotency-Key` reused with a different request body), `429` TOO_MANY_REQUESTS (first request for the same `Idempotency-Key` still processing; retry later with the same key), `6001` USER_ACCOUNT_UNAVAILABLE, `5005` CREATE_STRIPE_PAYMENT_INTENT_FAILED.

post.summary: Send message and open SSE stream
post.description:
Starts a new task on the instance and opens an SSE long connection. **Does not use the R<T> envelope.**
On success returns `text/event-stream`; if rejected returns JSON.
SSE event `type` is only `message` / `timeout` / `done`; after a `timeout` the server usually ends the SSE,
and the result can be retrieved via the offline-messages endpoint. Callers must check both the HTTP status code and `Content-Type`.

200.description: Stream opened, returns an SSE long connection

schema.description:
SSE event stream. Each `data` is one RelayMessage JSON (see the RelayMessage schema).
The event name matches the type: `message` / `timeout` / `done`.

400.description: Validation failed, or blocked by the high-risk sensitive-information-extraction filter

409.description: A task is already in progress (message_in_progress)

410.description: Instance no longer accepts messages (IDLE / PURGED)

get.description:
Reads Agent-visible messages from the offline queue once, when no SSE stream is connected. **Does not use the R<T> envelope.**
After reading, the queue is consumed; a subsequent call usually returns an empty array, so callers must persist results themselves.
Returns only `message` and `done` (startup placeholders are projected to message); `timeout` and ordinary process events are not returned.
When the instance does not exist or is not accessible, it likewise returns `{"messages": []}`.

post.description:
Re-attaches the SSE stream for a running task without sending a new message. **Does not use the R<T> envelope.**
On success returns `text/event-stream` (the first message has content `Reconnected.`, seq=0);
when there is no active task it returns `200 application/json` with `{"status":"no_active_task"}`.
Callers must handle both `text/event-stream` and `application/json`.

200.description: One of two forms: a successful reconnect SSE (text/event-stream), or a no-active-task JSON (application/json).

schema.description: SSE long connection; event structure matches RelayMessage

post.description:
Requests interruption of the instance's currently running task. **Does not use the R<T> envelope.**
A `200` only means the request was accepted; the task's terminal state still arrives via the existing SSE. Multiple interrupts are allowed.

Generates a temporary PUT presigned URL for a file to be uploaded. For user-side chat files, `bizType` is fixed to `buyer_agent`.
After obtaining `uploadUrl`, the caller PUTs the file binary directly and saves the returned `objectKey`.
Generates a temporary GET download URL from an existing `objectKey`. Currently only basic format validation is performed; object ownership is not checked.

default.description: Business error (invalid params, storage not enabled / config invalid / presign failed)

get.description: Returns the current user's instances grouped by status. `status` supports `active` (default) or `expired`.

patch.description: Renames the specified instance owned by the current user (1-50 chars; the server trims it).

default.description: Business error. Typical: `8001` INSTANCE_NOT_FOUND (not found or not owned), `400` BAD_REQUEST (name validation failed).

post.description:
Pays the storage fee for an instance in `idle` state (grace period) whose `purge_at` has not passed, extending its retention.
Always paid with `credit`, `2.00 credit` per month extending 30 days; `renewMonths` ranges 1-12.

default.description:
Business error. Typical: `8001` INSTANCE_NOT_FOUND, `8002` INSTANCE_RENEW_NOT_ALLOWED,
`5007` WALLET_CREDITS_AVAILABLE_NOT_ENOUGH, `429` TOO_MANY_REQUESTS, invalid params.

get.description: Returns the current user's subscriptions by status. `status` supports `active`/`retrying`/`canceled`/`expired`; omitting it returns all displayable states.

post.description:
Cancels auto-renewal (current period stays usable; no renewal after it ends). Only the user's own `active` subscription can be canceled.
Stripe subscriptions are synced to `cancel_at_period_end=true`; the local state updates only after the channel succeeds.

default.description:
Business error. Typical: `4029` SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND, `4039` SUBSCRIPTION_STATUS_INVALID,
`4040` SUBSCRIPTION_PROVIDER_SYNC_FAILED.

post.description:
Resumes auto-renewal for a "canceled but not yet expired" subscription, continuing the current period (no new period is started).
Only the user's own `canceled`, not-yet-expired subscription can be resumed.
Stripe subscriptions are synced to `cancel_at_period_end=false`; the local state updates only after the channel succeeds.

default.description:
Business error. Typical: `4029` SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND, `4039` SUBSCRIPTION_STATUS_INVALID,
`4040` SUBSCRIPTION_PROVIDER_SYNC_FAILED.

get.description:
Lists the historical versions of a Skill (only formally published versions whose package is still locatable), sorted by version number descending, not paginated.
Applies to Skills (download type) only, not hosted Agents. The `appVersionId` in each item can be used by download-related endpoints.

Gets the downloadable package URL for the published Skill version (download type only). Returns `data.skillInstall.url` (may be a presigned or raw HTTP URL).
Free plans can fetch it directly; paid plans require the current user to have purchased it.

default.description:
Business error. Typical: `2004` AGENT_NOT_FOUND, `2010` AGENT_NOT_AVAILABLE (incl. non-download / no package / no billing plan),
`4047` ORDER_DOWNLOAD_NOT_ALLOWED (paid Skill not purchased).

get.description:
Returns download info for an order: download orders return `skillInstall`, hosted orders return `thinSkillTemplate`.
Only `paid`/`completed` orders may download; links are temporary presigned URLs (~60 minutes), call again after expiry.

default.description:
Business error. Typical: `4019` ORDER_TYPE_NOT_SUPPORTED, `4003` ORDER_NOT_FOUND,
`4047` ORDER_DOWNLOAD_NOT_ALLOWED, `2024` AGENT_PACKAGE_NOT_FOUND.

get.description: Returns the current user's profile description; `profile` is `null` if not set.

put.description: Overwrites the current user's profile description; `profile` may be an empty string to clear it.

get.description:
Returns the current user's email, role (fixed `consumer`), credit balance, auto-consume thresholds (safety valve), and developer flag.
`autoConsume.monthlyUsed` is computed in real time from the orders table and cached for ~5 minutes.

BearerAccessToken.description: Access Token (prefix am_sk_), e.g. `Authorization: Bearer am_sk_xxx`

AccessTokenHeader.name: X-Access-Token
AccessTokenHeader.description: Access Token (prefix am_sk_), passed via the `X-Access-Token` header

OrderIdPath.name: orderId
OrderIdPath.description: Order ID / invoice ID (supports buy-/hou-/cre-/str- prefixes)

ApiVersion.name: Api-Version
ApiVersion.description: Optional. Pin behavior within the current major version, e.g. `2025-06-05`. Omit to use the latest stable behavior.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Optional. Prevents a retried/duplicated request (network jitter, timeout retry, double-click) from creating a duplicate order or charge. UUID v4 recommended. When provided, the server caches the first SUCCESSFUL response for 24h, keyed by (current user + this endpoint + Idempotency-Key); a retry with the same key returns that cached response instead of creating a new order. Reuse the same key only for retries of the same request; generate a new key for every new business request. Same key with a different request body within 24h -> `400 BAD_REQUEST`. If the first request with this key is still processing, the duplicate briefly waits and then returns `429 TOO_MANY_REQUESTS` (retry later with the same key). A failed first request releases the key so it can be retried.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized (token missing, invalid, expired, or revoked)

example.msg: Unauthorized: an Access Token is required

loginMethod.description: Login method; currently fixed to email

source.description: Fixed to agent in the Agent scope

accessToken.description: Agent-scope access token (prefix am_sk_). Persist this securely and send it as the Bearer token (or X-Access-Token) on every subsequent /agent/** request.

agentCard.description: Current display version card detail; fields per the actual response

creditScore.description: Seller credit score; defaults to 100 when no record

scoreBreakdown.description: Capafy score breakdown; structure per the external service

agentId.description: Required for a new order; may be omitted for renewal

hours.description: Required for hourly plans; must satisfy the plan's minPurchaseHours; omitted for subscription renewal

billingLineNo.description: Billing plan line number; effective only for new orders

instanceId.description: Passing it switches to renewal mode

orderId.description: Prefix buy-/hou-/cre-; subscriptions return the first-invoice orderId

status.description: Usually paid for successful credit payment

amount.description: Top-up amount (USD); service-layer minimum is 10 USD

content.description: The message body sent to the Agent this round; must not be blank

RelayMessage.description: Unified simplified structure for SSE / offline messages

originalQuestion.description: Returned on demand only when done

nextStepPlan.description: Returned on demand only when done

contentType.description: MIME type of the file to upload

bizType.description: Fixed to buyer_agent for user-side chat

headers.description: Headers that must accompany the upload; includes at least Content-Type

status.description: Fixed paid on the Agent side

cycle.description: daily / weekly / monthly; unmapped values returned as-is

url.description: Temporary download URL (presigned or raw HTTP)

profile.description: Profile description; an empty string clears it
